name: Potato Icebox Rolls (1954)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound baking potatoes, peeled and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the diced baking potatoes and enough water in a saucepan to cover. Bring to boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes until tender. Drain. Press through ricer, food mill, or sieve.
2. Dissolve the package of active dry yeast in 1/2 cup warm water (105°F to 115°F). Let stand 5 minutes until foamy. Heat 1 cup milk and 1/2 cup shortening in a saucepan over medium heat until bubbles form around the edge of the pan, then remove from heat. Cool slightly.
3. Beat the eggs, sugar, and salt in a mixer bowl at medium speed. Add the yeast mixture, milk mixture, and riced potatoes; beat until well blended. Gradually beat in 4 cups all-purpose flour. Stir in an additional 1 1/2 cups of flour with a wooden spoon.
4. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 8 to 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Knead in an additional 1/2 to 1 cup of flour if needed. Place the dough in a large greased bowl, turning to coat the top. Cover with a heavy plate or plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight or up to 48 hours.
5. About 2 1/2 hours before baking, punch down the dough. Grease 3 dozen 2 1/2-inch muffin pan cups or 3 cookie sheets. Divide the dough into 36 equal pieces; roll each piece into a ball and place it in the prepared muffin pans or on the cookie sheets. Cover with clean kitchen towels and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about 2 to 2 1/2 hours.
6. Preheat the oven to 425°F. Bake rolls for 12 to 15 minutes until golden brown.
